commands.repair_reason: Protected security label, dirty/unmergeable state, and unrelated CI/Docker/security changes require maintainer cleanup/review rather than an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: Needs attention because this external Google Chat auth PR carries unrelated CI/Docker/security-script changes, including a live Docker staging privacy regression.; Review finding: [P1] Restore the runner label script — scripts/ci-runner-... commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Google Chat auth transport to use `google-auth-library`'s bundled Gaxios, preserves native `Request` data through the guarded fetch path, adjusts Google Chat tests/dependencies/changelog, and also changes CI, Docker, release-check, security-rule, and assorted test helper scripts.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/77307 gives service-account steps and the `unsupported_grant_type` log, and current main routes Google Chat token creation through the custom guarded Gaxios transport. I did not run a live Google Chat service-account setup locally.
commands.review_followup: Protected `security` label, dirty/unmergeable state, and unrelated CI/Docker/security changes require maintainer cleanup/review rather than an automated repair lane.
commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
